source.title: Computational design and engineering of self-assembling multivalent microproteins with therapeutic potential against SARS-CoV-2

initial_request.research_problem: Need for broad-spectrum antivirals against evolving SARS-CoV-2 variants that evade existing monovalent drugs.
initial_request.known_context: SARS-CoV-2 mutations reduce efficacy of existing drugs and limit conserved epitopes; Omicron variants predominantly adopt 1-RBD-up conformation, evading many antibodies.
constraints: Design must achieve multivalent binding to all three RBDs
Constructs must self-assemble into stable trimers
Linkers must span calculated minimum distances
input_data: Structures of miniACE2, F-scaffold (T4 fibritin foldon), C-scaffold (coiled-coil)
quantifiable_goals: Broad neutralization of Omicron variants including cluster 2

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Existing monovalent drugs lose efficacy against SARS-CoV-2 variants due to mutations reducing conserved epitopes.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to achieve broad-spectrum neutralization despite epitope mutations.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Multivalent drugs can synergistically bind all three RBDs of the S protein, enhancing avidity and neutralization breadth.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Pursue computational design of self-assembling trivalent microproteins targeting the S protein.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Multivalent binding could overcome limitations of monovalent binders by increasing avidity and resisting mutations.

evidence.quote_or_span: the ongoing mutations in SARS-CoV-2 not only diminish the efficacy of existing drugs, but also reduce the number of conserved epitopes in drug targets, posing a significant challenge to drug design and development
there is an urgent need to develop novel therapeutic agents that can effectively combat emerging SARS-CoV-2 variants, such as broad-spectrum neutralizing antibodies or other protein drugs

multivalent drugs can use their pharmacophores (e.g., an antibody to a single RBD) to synergistically bind to the three RBDs of the S protein, thereby enhancing therapeutic effectiveness against the viruses

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis of multivalent binding established; need to select components and design linkers.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which monovalent binder and trimerization scaffolds to use, and how to connect them.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: miniACE2 as binder and F-/C-scaffolds as trimerization domains can yield self-assembling trimers if linkers span required distances.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Select miniACE2, F-scaffold (T4 fibritin foldon), C-scaffold (coiled-coil); calculate minimum linker distances.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Minimum linker distances: 19.37 Å for F-scaffold, 17.98 Å for C-scaffold.

evidence.quote_or_span: To obtain optimal multivalent constructs, we then selected two well-studied self-assembling domains as the test trimerization scaffolds, namely the $\beta$ -propeller-like foldon domain of T4 fibritin used in vaccines $[27–29]$ and an $\alpha$ -helical coiled-coil peptide $[30]$ , which will be referred to as F-scaffold and C-scaffold, respectively (Fig.

we calculated the minimum distance required for a linker to connect miniACE2 and the given trimerization scaffold using the Lagrange multiplier method, and found that the minimum distances for the F- and C-scaffolds are 19.37 Å and 17.98 Å, respectively

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Minimum linker distances known; need to select linker types and lengths.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which linker sequences and repeat numbers yield suitable distance distributions.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Flexible (GGGGS)n and rigid (EAAAK)n linkers with n=3 or 5 will provide appropriate reach and conformational sampling.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use RosettaRemodel to sample (GGGGS)n and (EAAAK)n for n=2-5; reject n=2 (too short) and n=4 (irregular distributions); select n=3 and 5.
evolution_trajectory.observation: n=2 linkers too short; n=4 gave irregular distance distributions; n=3 and 5 covered desired range. Selected four test linkers: (GGGGS)3, (GGGGS)5, (EAAAK)3, (EAAAK)5.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Selected linkers used to build constructs for MD evaluation.

evidence.quote_or_span: the distance distributions for n=4 were irregular, neither as narrow as n=3 nor as broad as n=5; furthermore, those for n=3 and 5 have covered most of the range seen in n=4, making n=4 less preferable
indicating that linkers designed with n=2 may not be suitable

the distance distributions for n=4 were irregular, neither as narrow as n=3 nor as broad as n=5; furthermore, those for n=3 and 5 have covered most of the range seen in n=4, making n=4 less preferable
we chose four test linkers, the flexible $(GGGGS)_{3}$ and $(GGGGS)_{5}$ , and the rigid $(EAAAK)_{3}$ and $(EAAAK)_{5}$

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Constructs with selected linkers built; need to evaluate self-assembly tendency.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which construct has the strongest self-assembly and most homogeneous conformation.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: MD simulations and MM/GBSA will rank constructs; F-scaffold constructs may be more stable.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Run 3×300 ns MD simulations for each construct; calculate MM/GBSA binding free energies.
evolution_trajectory.observation: F-scaffold constructs generally more stable; MP-5ff had lowest relative binding free energy, implying strongest self-assembly tendency.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: MP-5ff identified as top candidate; proceed to conformational analysis.

evidence.quote_or_span: the binding free energies of the F-scaffold constructs are typically lower than those of the C-scaffold constructs
MP-5ff has the lowest relative binding free energy, implying the strongest self-assembly tendency
All-atom MD simulations were performed using GROMACS software with AMBER 99SB-ILDN force field
For each construct, three independent 300-ns production runs were performed.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: MP-5ff identified as energetically favorable; need to assess conformational homogeneity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether MP-5ff adopts a single stable trimer conformation or multiple states.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: PCA-based free energy landscapes will reveal conformational distribution; MP-5ff may show a single narrow well.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Construct FELs from MD trajectories using PCA; compare MP-5ff with other constructs.
evolution_trajectory.observation: MP-5ff showed a single low-energy well, indicating high conformational homogeneity; other constructs had multiple wells.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: MP-5ff predicted to be best trimer; proceed to experimental validation.

evidence.quote_or_span: the resulting FELs showed the distribution patterns of the simulated constructs and their possible
only MP-5ff showed only a low-energy trimer conformation in the simulations, suggesting that the conformational homogeneity of this protein is the best.
except MP-5ff the FELs of all constructs had multiple low-energy wells (in blue), suggesting that multiple low-energy trimer-like conformations coexist in the simulations.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: MP-5ff computationally predicted as optimal; need experimental confirmation.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether MP-5ff indeed forms stable trimers with high affinity and neutralization.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: MP-5ff will show high trimerization efficiency, single conformation, and enhanced binding/neutralization over monovalent miniACE2.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Express and purify MP-5ff; characterize by SEC, Native-PAGE, BLI, and pseudovirus neutralization.

tool_or_method.name: SEC, Native-PAGE, BLI, pseudovirus neutralization assay

parameters.target: SARS-CoV-2 RBD (Wuhan-Hu-1 for BLI; pseudovirus for neutralization)

evolution_trajectory.observation: SEC: sharpest peak; Native-PAGE: single band; BLI: KD <1 pM (1000-fold over miniACE2); neutralization IC50 29 pM (23-fold improvement).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: MP-5ff validated; generalize trimerization unit to nanobody.

evidence.quote_or_span: the peak of MP-5ff appears to be the sharpest and the most concentrated one, indicating that it is the most efficient trivalent construct
only MP-5ff presented a single protein band, indicating a single stable trimer conformation
SEC profiles of the purified proteins of the constructs.
Native-PAGE analysis of the protein trimer fractions isolated from SEC.

5d, the monovalent miniACE2 was already able to inhibit the virus with an $IC_{50}$ of 682 pM; nonetheless, the trivalent MP-5ff still significantly enhanced the neutralizing activity ( $IC_{50}=29$ pM), exhibiting a 23-fold increase.

To obtain optimal multivalent constructs, we then selected two well-studied self-assembling domains as the test trimerization scaffolds, namely the $\beta$ -propeller-like foldon domain of T4 fibritin used in vaccines $[27–29]$ and an $\alpha$ -helical coiled-coil peptide $[30]$ , which will be referred to as F-scaffold and C-scaffold, respectively (Fig.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: MP-5ff trimerization unit (5ff) validated; want to test generalizability.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether the 5ff unit can be applied to a different binder (nanobody) to create a broad-spectrum antiviral.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Fusing Nb67 to 5ff will yield a trivalent nanobody (Tr67) with enhanced affinity and breadth.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design Tr67 by genetically fusing Nb67 to the 5ff trimerization unit.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Proceed to experimental characterization of Tr67.

evidence.quote_or_span: we applied the 5ff trimerization unit to engineer a trivalent nanobody targeting the dominant circulating Omicron variants

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Tr67 designed; need to assess trimerization, binding, and neutralization against Omicron BA.1.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether Tr67 forms stable trimers and shows improved potency over monovalent Nb67.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Tr67 will exhibit high trimerization efficiency, enhanced binding affinity, and strong neutralization of BA.1.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Express Tr67; characterize by SEC, Native-PAGE, BLI, and pseudovirus neutralization (BA.1).

tool_or_method.name: SEC, Native-PAGE, BLI, pseudovirus neutralization assay

evolution_trajectory.observation: SEC/Native-PAGE: high trimerization and homogeneity similar to MP-5ff; BLI: KD 0.746 nM (20-fold over Nb67); neutralization IC50 55 pM.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Tr67 shows promise; test breadth against more Omicron variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: SEC and Native-PAGE analysis of Tr67, showing high trimerization tendency and conformational homogeneity.
Tr67 has a trimerization efficiency and conformational homogeneity very similar to that of MP-5ff.
BLI measurement of the binding kinetics of the monovalent Nb67 and Tr67 to the immobilized RBD of SARS-CoV-2 pseudovirus (Omicron BA.1).
Neutralizing activities of Nb67 and Tr67 against SARS-CoV-2 pseudovirus (Omicron BA.1).
The resulting $K_{D}$ was 0.746 nM, which is an about 20-fold increase in affinity compared to that of the monovalent Nb67 ( $K_{D}=15.
746 nM, which is an about 20-fold increase in affinity compared to that of the monovalent Nb67 ( $K_{D}=15.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Tr67 neutralizes BA.1; need to test against broader panel of Omicron variants.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether Tr67 can neutralize Omicron variants that escape monovalent Nb67, especially cluster 2.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Tr67 will broadly neutralize Omicron variants, including cluster 2, due to avidity effects.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test Tr67 and Nb67 against pseudoviruses of BA.5, BF.7, BQ.1.1, XBB.1, XBB.1.5.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Tr67 neutralized all variants (IC50: 0.5-14.6 nM); Nb67 failed against cluster 2.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Broad neutralization achieved; determine structural basis.

evidence.quote_or_span: Tr67 has a significant increase in the neutralizing activity against all the tested Omicron variants
Nb67 was able to neutralize the Omicron variants in cluster 1 but not those in cluster 2

Thus, compared with its monovalent counterpart, Tr67 has a significant increase in the neutralizing activity against all the tested Omicron variants, suggesting that multivalent proteins have the potential to be developed into broad-spectrum drugs against the emerging SARS-CoV-2 variants.
for variants BA.5, BF.7, BQ.1.1, XBB.1, and XBB.1.5 variants (cluster 2), Nb67 in the best-scoring poses was not located at the expected sites

1.5 variants (cluster 2), Nb67 in the best-scoring poses was not located at the expected sites, but at other sites that are sterically unfavorable in the 1-RBD-up conformation of the S protein (Additional file 1: Fig.
1.5, unlike most neutralizing antibodies, which lose potency against these variants $[5, 47, 48]$ .

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Tr67 shows broad neutralization; need structural confirmation of binding mode.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether Tr67 indeed binds all three RBDs in 3-RBD-up conformation as designed.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Cryo-EM will reveal Tr67 bound to Omicron BA.1 spike in 3-RBD-up state.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform cryo-EM on Tr67–Omicron BA.1 spike complex.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 3D reconstruction at 9 Å confirmed Tr67 binds all three RBDs, inducing 3-RBD-up conformation.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Structure obtained; analyze interfaces to explain broad neutralization.

evidence.quote_or_span: the cryo-EM structure provided experimental evidence that Tr67 is indeed bound to the epitopes specified by the computational design
the cryo-EM structure did confirm that Tr67 can induce the Omicron spike into the 3-RBD-up conformation
we captured the structure of the Omicron BA.1 spike protein in the 3-RBD-up conformation, and thus provided mechanistic insights into the antiviral activity of the trivalent nanobody Tr67.
1 spike protein in the 3-RBD-up conformation, and thus provided mechanistic insights into the antiviral activity of the trivalent nanobody Tr67.
This not only results in wider binding interfaces (e.g., in Fig. 8e), but also ensures that all three bound binders (Nb67s) in Tr67 remain tightly associated with the RBDs.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Cryo-EM structure available; need to rationalize broad neutralization and Nb67 escape.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Why Tr67 neutralizes cluster 2 variants while Nb67 fails.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Tr67 has larger interface and more interactions; Nb67 cannot dock to cluster 2 RBDs at expected epitope due to mutations.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze interface residues and perform docking simulations of Nb67 to cluster 2 RBDs.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Tr67 interface: 21 Nb67 residues, 25 RBD residues (vs. 14, 16 for monovalent). Nb67 docking to cluster 2 RBDs placed at non-canonical sites.

evidence.quote_or_span: the Nb67 binder in Tr67 has a larger contact area with the Omicron RBD
the number of interfacial residues also supports that Tr67 could establish a more extensive network of interactions
for variants BA.5, BF.7, BQ.1.1, XBB.1, and XBB.1.5 variants (cluster 2), Nb67 in the best-scoring poses was not located at the expected sites
the synergistic binding of the three Nb67s in Tr67 increases the binding interface and then leads to higher binding affinities that could resist the mutations
fused protein of gp41 NHR to the T4 fibritin trimerization domain to construct trimeric anti-HIV-1 therapeutics [25].

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Tr67 validated in vitro; need to state limitations and future work.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: In vivo efficacy, pharmacokinetics, immunogenicity, and biosafety not evaluated.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Further studies required to assess therapeutic potential.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Acknowledge that study is limited to computational design and in vitro validation.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Study focused on design and in vitro validation; in vivo data needed.

evidence.quote_or_span: our study mainly focused on the computational design of a series of self-assembling multivalent microproteins and the in vitro validation of their physicochemical properties, improved binding affinity, and neutralizing activity
further research is needed to investigate the in vivo therapeutic efficacy, pharmacokinetics, immunogenicity, and biosafety of Tr67

success_verification.final_conclusion: Computationally designed trivalent microprotein MP-5ff and its nanobody derivative Tr67 achieved sub-nanomolar binding, broad neutralization of Omicron variants including cluster 2, and induced 3-RBD-up conformation, validating the multivalent design strategy.
limitations: No in vivo therapeutic efficacy data
Pharmacokinetics, immunogenicity, and biosafety not evaluated
MM/GBSA has limitations in absolute binding free energy prediction
